title: Live Action Sequence and other questions about EoE
display_title: Live Action Sequence and other questions about EoE
wikitext:
[]: Live-Action Sequence and other questions about EoE             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Jun 12, 2010 10:39 pm I already posted this on the imdb.com forum for EoE and a user directed me to this forum and I'm very happy to join. It's been a long time since something has affected me as Evangelion has. I would like to use this thread to discuss things that come to my mind about the film and the series. Anyway, here is my post as seen on imdb.com The live-action bit has a part where the camera is panning above a theater auditorium. When was this shot? I ask because it's a packed theater and I think that a member of the audience is holding a Rei doll. I've heard that this was the theater where the film premiered, I'm very curious what the effect of those shots might have been like on the audience! Did anyone see this in the theater? If so, how did you and the audience react to the film? I ask because this film left me absolutely floored from beginning to end. When the entire world turned into LCL and you could hear everybody screaming, I had to pause the scene and leave the room a bit. It was just really hard hitting, especially after seeing so many of the characters being killed off and being horribly frustrated at Shinji's decision to basically end the world. The people who saw it in the theater, were, for the most part if not entirely people who were following the series up until that point. It must've been heartbreaking to see so many beloved characters and the whole world die. Sorry if I'm just speaking nonsense, but it's been a long time since a film has hit me like this one did. It truly is a work of art, as well as the entire series. I love it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jun 12, 2010 11:07 pm Howdy. It was me who directed you to here. I guess we can just wait and see who replies. :)

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Jun 12, 2010 11:33 pm Now you all know who to thank. Thanks, Eva. Glad to be a part of this forum now.

[]: Re: Live-Action Sequence and other questions about EoE             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jun 13, 2010 12:14 am shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: The live-action bit has a part where the camera is panning above a theater auditorium. When was this shot? I ask because it's a packed theater and I think that a member of the audience is holding a Rei doll. I've heard that this was the theater where the film premiered

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Aug 07, 2010 4:33 am Okay, back to this thread, but there is one question that has been bugging me for a while and I searched and it seems like nobody really discusses it, but: What are the green crosses? Do they mean anything in particular? The only way I have been able to interpret them are as some sort of "marker" that indicates that the souls of a certain place have been taken. Also, the copy I have of EoE has something written in Japanese after the "Thanatos" credits in the middle of the film, what does it say? Since my DVD has no translation. I've heard it's some sort of dedicatee.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Aug 07, 2010 9:22 am It's never been stated exactly what the green crosses represent, but our best guess is that they are some sort of artifact of or manifestation of the Anti A.T.-Field. Perhaps they appear in large population centers where many A.T. Fields need to be supressed. Any theory would involve speculation. Even the script simply calls them "green crosses". There is absolutely no supporting evidence for some of the wilder theories, like the one from the EOE commentary that claims they are extra Angels exploding.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 07, 2010 11:46 am shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: Also, the copy I have of EoE has something written in Japanese after the "Thanatos" credits in the middle of the film, what does it say? Since my DVD has no translation. I've heard it's some sort of dedicatee.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Aug 07, 2010 2:32 pm Thanks, guys! I'm hoping to see the entire series and the films again soon, I'm sure I'll have a lot more questios when that happens.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Sat Aug 07, 2010 10:26 pm Actually, I think that the audience was very confused troughout the whole thing. First they are watching Rebirth, whitch they might've not known was actually the first part of Air, I don't know if it was common knowlege. Then the credits roll and it wouldn't surprise me if people walked off not knowing it was only half way done, then I'm sure someone told them that it wasn't over and they go back in with the people that stayed for the whole credits to see if it's true, which it is, and they are now uncertain of how things will go from that point on. Then, after they watch a very confusing and emotionally disturbing movie there is suddenly a 'The End' and the light go on. At that point I wouldn't know what to do, get up, go, stay and wait, applaud, laugh, cry, punch the guy next to me, piss myself in anger, ask for my money back, find Anno and slap him, all of the above..? What tha' fuck!!!???

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Aug 08, 2010 9:16 pm berto wrote: Actually, I think that the audience was very confused troughout the whole thing. First they are watching Rebirth, whitch they might've not known was actually the first part of Air, I don't know if it was common knowlege. Then the credits roll and it wouldn't surprise me if people walked off not knowing it was only half way done, then I'm sure someone told them that it wasn't over and they go back in with the people that stayed for the whole credits to see if it's true, which it is, and they are now uncertain of how things will go from that point on. Then, after they watch a very confusing and emotionally disturbing movie there is suddenly a 'The End' and the light go on. At that point I wouldn't know what to do, get up, go, stay and wait, applaud, laugh, cry, punch the guy next to me, piss myself in anger, ask for my money back, find Anno and slap him, all of the above..? What tha' fuck!!!???

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 09, 2010 2:24 pm But the commentary's theory of the extra Angels is the one that makes the most sense.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Tue Dec 14, 2010 5:53 pm So I'm near halfway through EoE again and I have to ask: Did Misato know that the EVA Mass Production series had S2 Engine Cores? And if she did, why didn't she tell Asuka to destroy the cores? And does destroying the cores actually kill the MP EVAs? Also, Fuyutsuki mentions that The Tree of Life formed by the EVA 01, GNR and the MP EVAs could become an Ark to save humanity from Third Impact. How would that have worked?

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Tue Dec 14, 2010 7:29 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: So I'm near halfway through EoE again and I have to ask: Did Misato know that the EVA Mass Production series had S2 Engine Cores? And if she did, why didn't she tell Asuka to destroy the cores? And does destroying the cores actually kill the MP EVAs?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Dec 14, 2010 10:20 pm BiQ wrote: She could be totally oblivious to their regenerative abilities even if she knew they had S2 inside.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Tue Dec 14, 2010 11:28 pm True. She probably also assumed that Shinji would actually get out there in time with Unit 01, since it had an S2 engine as well. She probably had Asuka fight them to buy time.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Dec 15, 2010 12:17 am Thanks for the replies, they seem to be reasonable explanations. I must add that after a second full viewing, that I truly find EoE to be a masterpiece.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 15, 2010 3:38 pm By the way, destroying the cores would kill an Eva regardless of whether they have an S2 Engine or not; it's where the soul is. So Asuka was being sort of stupid anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 15, 2010 3:57 pm Come to think of it, I didn't see a core on any of the Mass Produced Evas. Perhaps the lack of core/lack of soul was intentional?

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed Dec 15, 2010 4:08 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Come to think of it, I didn't see a core on any of the Mass Produced Evas. Perhaps the lack of core/lack of soul was intentional?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 15, 2010 4:46 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: But they do have one, it's the thing they connect with their Lances during Third Impact. Although when we see them in battle mode, we can't see them at all so it's reasonable to expect why couldn't Asuka see them. But still, seeing as they were Evas, it would still have been reasonable for any of them to expect that they had a core.
